@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned SLK Kompressor engine typically costs £1,800–£8,500 supply only depending on generation. M111 engines (R170) sit at the lower end (£1,800–£5,500), while M271 units (R171) run £2,500–£8,500. A full engine replacement can run anywhere between £1,500 and £5,000. A timing chain repair on the M271 typically costs £1,200–£2,500. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on workshop location. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine generation your SLK has. The M111 (R170, 1997–2004) is generally reliable but has age-related timing and oil leak issues. The M271 (R171, 2004–2011) is a 'rather good engine otherwise' but is notorious for timing chain stretch — some specialists recommend avoiding any M271-powered car without documented repairs. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor?

acceptedAnswer.text: M271 timing chain stretch (R171 — rattling on startup, potential catastrophic failure), M111 timing chain and camshaft adjuster issues (R170 — rattling when revved), oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and camshaft adjuster magnets, M111 supercharger clutch wear, MAF sensor failure on M111, and carbon build-up on M271 intake valves.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz SLK Kompressor engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold: if your SLK is worth £5,000–£10,000 and a recon engine is £3,500–£5,000 (35–100% of the car's value), assess the overall condition carefully. Well-maintained examples with good bodywork and interior justify replacement spend. Tired examples with significant rust or roof issues may not. R170 models are becoming classics — a good engine rebuild can add value if the rest of the car is in good condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: R170 (1997–2000) SLK 200 is M111 E20 (2.0L I4, 136 PS); R170 (2000–2004) SLK 200 Kompressor is M111 E20 ML (2.0L I4 SC, 163 PS); R170 (1997–2000) SLK 230 Kompressor is M111 E23 ML (2.3L I4 SC, 193 PS); R170 (2000–2004) SLK 230 Kompressor is M111 E23 ML (2.3L I4 SC, 197 PS); R171 (2004–2008) SLK 200 Kompressor is M271 E18 ML (1.8L I4 SC, 163 PS); R171 (2008–2011) SLK 200 Kompressor is M271 E18 ML (1.8L I4 SC, 184 PS). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M271 timing chain, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,200–£2,500) is far cheaper than catastrophic failure requiring a full engine replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Some specialists recommend replacing the timing gear as soon as you buy an M271-powered car. Regular oil changes with the correct specification are essential for timing chain longevity.

eraMap.title: The SLK KOMPRESSOR THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: 136 PS — naturally aspirated, robust but M111 timing and MAF issues possible
163 PS — supercharged M111, timing chain and supercharger clutch concerns
193 PS — the original SLK engine, robust but age-related timing issues
197 PS — facelift version, same M111 vulnerabilities
163 PS — early M271, timing chain stretch from ~100,000 km
184 PS — later M271 EVO, revised but still timing chain issues

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market rates. A full engine replacement can run anywhere between £1,500 and £5,000, with Mercedes engines typically at the higher end of the range. Reconditioned engines typically cost £1,200–£2,200 supply only for standard units, with total installed costs around £1,800–£2,800. M111 units generally sit at the lower end of the range; M271 units command a premium. Used M271 engines are available from specialist suppliers. A timing chain repair on the M271 typically costs £1,200–£2,500.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SLK Kompressor engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The M271's supercharger and timing chain complexity add labour time compared to naturally aspirated engines. The R171's compact engine bay makes access more challenging than the R170.

cta.label: → Compare SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: M271 Timing Chain Stretch & Sprocket Wear (R171 2004–2011)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, rough engine running, loss of power, engine warning light, camshaft timing faults. The timing chain can stretch, and sprockets can wear, causing the chain to skip a tooth. A leaking hydraulic tensioner can cause rattling on every startup, not just cold starts. In the worst case, the chain can jump, causing catastrophic engine damage.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — early M271 engines typically fail around 100,000 miles, while later versions can fail from 30,000 miles.
problems.repairCost: £1,200–£2,500 (chain, tensioners, sprockets). If the engine is damaged, costs increase significantly.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on startup, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The M271's timing chain is its most critical weakness. Early engines use a simplex chain that is more prone to wear. If you hear any rattling on startup, book an inspection immediately — preventative chain replacement is far cheaper than a full engine rebuild. Some specialists recommend replacing the timing gear as soon as you buy an M271-powered car. Regular oil changes with the correct specification are essential for timing chain longevity.

problems.title: M111 Timing Chain & Camshaft Adjuster Issues (R170 1997–2004)
problems.symptoms: Rattling when the engine is revved, engine warning light with camshaft position faults, sluggish acceleration, rough idle. The variable timing gear can fail, or the timing chain tensioner and sprockets can wear. Oil leaks from camshaft adjuster magnets can travel through the wiring harness to the ECU.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling when revving, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The M111 is generally reliable, but timing chain issues can occur. The cam magnet mechanism can leak oil into the wiring loom — a modified piggyback loom from Mercedes (£20) can prevent ECU damage. If you hear rattling when revving, have the timing advance mechanism inspected immediately. A failing MAF sensor can also cause sluggish acceleration — never use cheap pattern parts.

problems.title: M111 & M271 Oil Leaks
problems.symptoms: Visible oil stains under the vehicle, burning oil smell from the engine bay, oil residue on the engine block, gradual oil level decrease. The M111 commonly leaks from the valve cover gasket and camshaft adjuster solenoid. The M271 also suffers from oil leaks at the valve cover gasket, oil filter housing and oil cooler.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, oil loss can lead to engine damage.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are a common age-related issue on both M111 and M271 engines. Regular inspection and timely replacement of gaskets and seals are essential to prevent larger problems. When replacing the valve cover gasket, also inspect and replace spark plug seals to prevent oil intrusion into spark plug wells. On the M111, if oil leaks from the camshaft adjuster magnet, fit the modified piggyback loom to prevent ECU damage.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Catch early; modified loom prevents ECU damage
Common maintenance — repair when symptoms appear
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SLK Kompressor? Check These First
buyIf: * R170 M111 with full service history and no timing chain rattle — robust and proven
* R171 M271 with documented timing chain replacement — the critical repair done
* M111 with cam magnet modified loom fitted — prevents ECU damage
* Full service history with regular oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
avoidIf: * R171 M271 with cold-start rattle — timing chain stretch is imminent
* R170 M111 with rattling when revved — timing adjuster or chain issues
* Any engine with oil leaks or burning smell — gaskets are deteriorating
* Poor service history on any generation — these engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The SLK Kompressor range spans two generations of Mercedes engines — the M111 (R170, 1997–2004) and the M271 (R171, 2004–2011). The M111 is generally reliable but has age-related timing and oil leak issues. The M271 is a "rather good engine otherwise" but is notorious for timing chain stretch — some specialists recommend avoiding any M271-powered car without documented repairs. With the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold, a £3,500–£5,000 reconditioned engine on an SLK valued at £5,000–£10,000 is 35–100% of the car's value — assess the overall condition carefully. Well-maintained examples with documented timing chain work justify replacement spend. R170 models are becoming classics; a good engine rebuild can add value if the rest of the car is in good condition.

cta.label: → Compare SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz SLK 200 / 230 Kompressor (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start (M271) — Listen for rattling on startup (timing chain stretch warning)
* Rev Test (M111) — Listen for rattling when revved (timing adjuster or chain issues)
* Service History — Including which engine era (M111/M271) and oil-interval discipline
* Timing Chain Documentation — For M271, evidence of chain replacement is essential
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover, oil filter housing and oil cooler
* Cam Magnet — On M111, check for oil leaks from camshaft adjuster magnet
* Supercharger — Listen for whining or grinding noises (M111 Kompressor clutch)
* MAF Sensor — Test acceleration for sluggishness (MAF failure is common on M111)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
